title: gratiola pedunculata
intro: Gratiola pedunculata, also known as Marsh Felwort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Gratiola pedunculata include its ability to reduce fever, treat digestive problems, and alleviate anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Gratiola pedunculata can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
